federal_land_actions.description: Convey the federal Red Rock Ridge tract out of National Forest System ownership subject to access and resource-protection reservations.
Acquire the non-federal meadow and riparian tract for National Forest System consolidation and watershed management.
Reserve trail, administrative access, and riparian monitoring rights needed after closing.

proposed_action_elements.description: Exchange federal upland parcels for a non-federal meadow parcel with Forest Plan consistency and realty case review.

evidence_refs.citation_label: Red Rock Ridge proposed action paragraph 1
evidence_refs.summary: Proposed federal disposal, non-federal acquisition, and Forest Plan consistency context.
evidence_refs.title: Red Rock Ridge Land Exchange Proposed Action

proposed_action_elements.description: Reserve public trail and administrative access easements across the exchange parcels.

evidence_refs.citation_label: Red Rock Ridge proposed action paragraph 2
evidence_refs.summary: Proposed trail and administrative access reservations.
evidence_refs.title: Red Rock Ridge Land Exchange Proposed Action

proposed_action_elements.description: Apply riparian monitoring and wetland protection conditions to the acquired meadow tract.

evidence_refs.citation_label: Red Rock Ridge proposed action paragraph 3
evidence_refs.summary: Proposed riparian monitoring, wetland protection, and water-quality conditions.
evidence_refs.title: Red Rock Ridge Land Exchange Proposed Action

proposed_action_elements.description: Coordinate cultural-resource survey, SHPO review, and tribal consultation for the exchange parcels.

evidence_refs.citation_label: Red Rock Ridge proposed action paragraph 4
evidence_refs.summary: Proposed cultural-resource, SHPO, and tribal consultation work.
evidence_refs.title: Red Rock Ridge Land Exchange Proposed Action

proposed_action_summary: The Forest Service proposes the Red Rock Ridge Land Exchange to convey federal upland parcels, acquire a non-federal meadow and riparian tract, reserve public and administrative access, and complete wetland, water-quality, cultural-resource, and tribal consultation work before closing.

proposed_action_basis: The exchange requires a lands/realty case file for federal disposal and non-federal acquisition.
The exchange must be screened against the applicable Forest Plan.
Reserved access easements and trail commitments trigger roads, trails, and access analysis.
Riparian and wetland protection conditions trigger wetlands and floodplain screening.
Riparian monitoring and water-resource conditions trigger water-quality review.
Survey and SHPO coordination trigger cultural-resource analysis.
Tribal consultation commitments trigger tribal-relations planning requirements.
